context: Tests whether the agent uses upsert semantics for exposure deduplication, gates conversion tracking on prior exposure, applies the chi-squared test with the specific p-value approximation formula, reports relative lift, and surfaces sample size sufficiency in the results API.

checklist.name: Exposure upsert
checklist.description: trackExposure uses an upsert operation (not a plain insert) keyed on experimentId + userId to prevent double-counting when the same user is exposed multiple times

checklist.name: Conversion exposure gate
checklist.description: trackConversion checks for an existing exposure record before recording the conversion, and returns/exits early if no exposure is found

checklist.name: Chi-squared test used
checklist.description: calculateSignificance uses a chi-squared (not z-test, t-test, or Fisher exact) approach to compute statistical significance

checklist.name: p-value approximation formula
checklist.description: Uses the specific formula Math.exp(-0.717 * chiSquared - 0.416 * chiSquared * chiSquared) to derive the p-value from the chi-squared statistic

checklist.name: Relative lift calculation
checklist.description: Reports relative lift as (variantCVR - controlCVR) / controlCVR — not absolute difference

checklist.name: Significance threshold
checklist.description: Flags results as statistically significant when pValue < 0.05

checklist.name: Sample size sufficiency
checklist.description: Results response includes whether the current sample size has reached the required minimum (a 'reached' or equivalent flag)

checklist.name: Results API structure
checklist.description: GET /api/experiments/:id/results handler returns control stats, variant stats, and significance information in a single response

checklist.name: analysis-notes mentions novelty
checklist.description: analysis-notes.md mentions reporting results with and without early data (first few days) to detect novelty effects

checklist.name: analysis-notes mentions stopping rule
checklist.description: analysis-notes.md explicitly warns against stopping the test early and mentions using pre-calculated sample size as the stopping rule
